notes: A real form-library C program -- new_form over new_field fields with initial buffers, set_form_win/sub, post_form, then form_driver data entry (with O_BLANK first-edit blanking), REQ_DEL_PREV editing, and REQ_NEXT/PREV_FIELD navigation, each wrefresh'd -- renders byte-identically to the system libformw when linked against the native libncurses_cabi. post_form draws each field's buffer (padded to the field width, in the field back attribute) into the form (sub)window; form_driver edits the current field's buffer and parks the cursor at the edit position; the byte-exact doupdate renders the diff.
oracle_method: compile one form C program twice -- linked to the native libncurses_cabi.a and to the system libformw+libncursesw -- run both under an 80x24 xterm pty and compare the rendered/edited form body
